sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after SPACE. Looking at the image, I can see a blue square appeared above the green player where the charcoal frame was. The frame seems to have been "opened" or "collected". Let me check the current state to understand what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print(f"Nodes: {len(seg)}")
for n in seg:
    b = (min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']), min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']))
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, pos={b}, children={n['children']}")

# Check green player position
green = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
gb = (min(p[0] for p in green['boundary']), min(p[1] for p in green['boundary']))
print(f"\nGreen at: {gb}")

# Check what's adjacent to green
adj = current_frame.segmentation['adjacency_list']
green_id = green['id']
for pair in adj:
    if green_id in pair:
        other = pair[0] if pair[1] == green_id else pair[1]
        other_node = seg[other]
        print(f"Green adj: node {other}, color={other_node['color']}, pixels={other_node['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After pressing SPACE, the charcoal frame (node 7, previously 'c' with 12 pixels) changed to white 'W' with 16 pixels. The blue square inside (node 8) is still there. The player moved down to (41, 32) - away from the frame. The frame is now white instead of charcoal, and the player is adjacent to it.
It seems like SPACE "opened" the frame (changed from charcoal to white) and the player moved back down. The blue square is now accessible. Let me try moving UP again to get into the white frame and collect the blue square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Frame turned white after SPACE. Player at (41,32), white frame at (36,32).
# Try moving UP to enter the opened frame and collect the blue square
action(['UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
